questions.question: Prove that the set of all 2×2 symmetric matrices forms a subspace of M₂ₓ₂.
expectedPoints: Verify the set contains the zero matrix (which is symmetric)
Show closure under addition: if A and B are symmetric, then A+B is symmetric
Show closure under scalar multiplication: if A is symmetric, then kA is symmetric for any scalar k

questions.question: Let V be the vector space of all polynomials with real coefficients. Determine whether the set W = {p(x) ∈ V | p(0) = 0 and p(1) = 0} is a subspace of V, and justify your answer.
expectedPoints: Check if the zero polynomial satisfies p(0)=0 and p(1)=0
Verify closure under addition: if p,q ∈ W, then (p+q)(0)=p(0)+q(0)=0 and (p+q)(1)=p(1)+q(1)=0
Verify closure under scalar multiplication: if p ∈ W and c is a scalar, then (cp)(0)=c·p(0)=0 and (cp)(1)=c·p(1)=0
Conclude that W is a subspace since it satisfies all three conditions of the subspace test
Optional: Note that W consists of all polynomials divisible by x(x-1)
